summary: Website that discovers pickup lines from Instagram (excluding one-liners) and explains how users can use them. Rename product to RizzAssist and add the ability to fetch/add lines directly from Instagram (not only user-submitted URLs/text).
architectural_notes: Instagram auto-search/import may require handling platform restrictions (auth, rate limits) and/or using an official API; scraping may be unreliable and risky.
known_issues: Build/deploy pipeline hit an internal configuration error; user requested a rerun.
Some added UI images/decorations show visible 'vector' artifacts/outlines; needs cleanup/asset/style fix.
Secondary color/theme accent is not the intended dark red (as per reference image); needs adjustment to match dark red.
imageRequirements:
## Edit Images
- Improve the logo artwork (rizzassist-logo.dim_512x256.png > rizzassist-logo.dim_512x256.png)

existing_features.title: Create a web app that lets users discover multi-line pickup lines that are sourced from Instagram content, by allowing users to submit an Instagram post URL and/or paste the pickup line text from the post.

Enforce a rule that one-line pickup lines are rejected: only accept pickup lines that contain multiple lines of dialogue or multiple lines separated by line breaks (e.g., at least 2 line breaks or at least 3 non-empty lines).
For each stored pickup line, generate and display a "How to use it" guide in English that explains delivery, timing/context, and a suggested follow-up message, without using external AI/LLM services.
Implement pickup line browsing features: a feed/list view, a random pickup line action, and basic search (by keyword).
Add basic content moderation controls to reduce low-quality entries: prevent empty/whitespace submissions, cap maximum length, and provide a report button per pickup line (stored as a flag/count).
Apply a cohesive, creative visual theme for the app (colors, typography, layout) with a distinct playful style appropriate for a pickup-line discovery site, and use it consistently across all pages.
Add and render generated static image assets for branding (logo and a small decorative illustration) from frontend/public/assets/generated.
Resolve the internal configuration error in the build/deploy pipeline and rerun the build so the latest app version successfully builds and deploys.
Rename the app/product to "RizzAssist" across the frontend UI and metadata, including (at minimum) the header brand alt text and the HTML document title.

Revamp the overall visual design of the RizzAssist frontend to look significantly more polished, complex, and "beautiful" while keeping all existing functionality (browse/search/random/submit/detail/report) intact.

Add more decorative static image assets (illustrations/patterns/stickers) and incorporate them into the UI across pages (e.g., header/hero, feed empty state, submit page side decoration, and/or footer) to create a more visually dense experience.

Upgrade the feed presentation to feel more premium and information-rich (without adding new backend data): enhance the pickup line cards and surrounding UI with improved typography, spacing, subtle background treatments, and more engaging loading/empty states that use the new imagery.

Enhance the submit view and the pickup-line detail modal to match the upgraded aesthetic (e.g., add a decorative header area, richer section styling, and optional side illustration) while keeping form validation and report actions unchanged.

Improve the existing RizzAssist logo artwork by updating the generated logo image to a more visually striking, more colorful version while keeping the same filename and dimensions so it renders everywhere it is currently used.

Increase the overall vibrancy of the site’s color palette (while preserving readability) by updating the global theme tokens and key gradient/colored UI surfaces used across the app (header, background, hero sections, buttons/icons) to use more saturated accent and primary colors consistent with the existing playful style.

Remove the “random side things” decorative elements across the UI by deleting or disabling non-essential decorative layers (corner flourishes, sticker accents, and similar side/corner decorations) in the layout, header, feed cards, submit page, and pickup-line detail modal, while keeping core content/layout intact.

Increase the overall vibrancy and amount of color in the UI (background gradients, primary/secondary/accent usage, and key surfaces) while preserving text readability and contrast in both light and dark modes.

Eliminate visible “vector” artifacts/outlines in rendered decorative images by updating image rendering/styling (e.g., remove pixelated/crisp-edge rendering) and/or replacing affected generated image assets with clean, anti-aliased versions that do not show vector edges.

Replace the current RizzAssist logo image used in the header with a new logo artwork while keeping the exact same asset filename and dimensions so it continues to render everywhere it is currently referenced (e.g., in AppHeader).

Update the app’s secondary theme color to a clearly dark red that visually matches the user-provided reference image, and ensure this dark red is consistently used anywhere the secondary/accent color is intended across the UI (including gradients and highlighted surfaces).

Remove or refactor any hard-coded non-theme red/pink/orange gradient color stops currently used for key background/hero gradients so that they derive from the updated theme tokens (primary/secondary/accent) and stay consistent after the dark red change.

Update the app’s secondary theme color to exactly match the dark red shade from the user-provided reference picture by adjusting the theme tokens in `frontend/src/index.css` for both `:root` and `.dark` (at minimum `--secondary`, and any related foreground token if required for readability), so all UI surfaces that use `secondary` (including gradient stops like `via-secondary/...` and highlight backgrounds like `to-secondary/...`) render with the exact intended shade.

Replace the existing generated RizzAssist logo image with a higher-quality, more visually striking logo while keeping the exact same filename and dimensions so it continues to render everywhere it is referenced (including the header image at `/assets/generated/rizzassist-logo.dim_512x256.png`).

new_feature_requests.summary: Build a website that finds pickup lines from Instagram and provides guidance on how to use them, excluding one-line pickup lines.
Add functionality to search Instagram and import/add pickup lines directly (system-sourced), rather than relying only on user-submitted post URLs or pasted text.
Rename the app/product to "RizzAssist" across the UI/branding (title, headers, metadata, logo wordmark as needed).

Add a non-Instagram-API way to have the app include pickup lines beyond user-submitted entries by seeding the backend with an initial set of multi-line pickup lines at deployment (system-provided), while keeping the existing multi-line validation rules.
Improve the UI/visual design to look more complex and beautiful, including adding more images/visual elements across the site.
Improve the RizzAssist logo design and update the site color palette to be more vibrant/colourful.
Remove unwanted/random side decorative elements and clean up image rendering so vector/artifact outlines are not visible.
Change/update the RizzAssist logo (refresh the logo artwork/wordmark).
